texto: The love-goddess gestured to the fish of the lake below her and they gleefully gave up their scales to clothe her in a glimmering gown. The very ends of her toes dangled like cherries over the water as she drifted toward the beach. Even her pointed finger seemed a welcome sight. With a voice that sounded like the wind though roses, she whispered, ‘My boy . . .
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a I. Wright: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. O trecho “The love-goddess gestured to the fish of the lake below her and they gleefully gave up their scal…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que amor, aphrodite, love pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de termino e love.
